column4: Appendix 1. Details of reported baleen whale injury events along the Gulf of Mexico, US East Coast and Atlantic Canadian provinces, 2019-2023. Assignment of Serious Injury (SI) criteria, SI values and List of Fisheries (LOF) inclusion follow the guidance established by NMFS Instruction 02-038-01.

column11: Description

column4: Western North Atlantic right whale (Eubalaena glacialis)
column11: # events/sums:

Free-swimming with closed bridle through mouth, lines trailing 1 body length, and evidence of health decline. Partially disentangled. Confirmed gear-free in October 2019. Resights show improved health.
No gear present. Evidence of new, extensive raw and healing injuries on peduncle and leading fluke edges. Resights indicate healing.

column6: 24.2 nm E of Miscou Island, NB
column11: Free-swimming with line through mouth trailing back to constricting fluke insertion wraps. Partially disentangled, line between mouth and fluke cut, shed remaining gear. Resights indicate healing.
No gear present. Evidence of recent multiple constricting rostrum wraps along with injuries to lips and at fluke insertion. Resights indicate healing. Event added in 2018-2022 report.

Free-swimming with extensive entanglement involving embedded head wraps, flipper wraps, and trailing gear. Baleen damaged and protruding from mouth. Partially disentangled: 200-300 ft. of line removed. Embedded rostrum and blowhole wraps remain, but now able to open mouth. Significant health decline. No resights.
No gear present but evidence of constricting entanglement across rostrum and on peduncle and fluke insertion. Resights indicate healing. Previously reported as 22-Jan-2020 in Cape Cod Bay.

Free-swimming with 2-3 lines exiting both sides of the mouth, joining in a jumble of line approximately 1 body length aft of whale and trailing. Loose wraps only. Resights confirm shed gear and injuries healing.

column6: 7 nm E of Altamaha Sound, GA
column11: Dependent calf with deep lacerations to head and lips, exposing bone. No resights post-15-Jan-2020.
No gear present but evidence of constricting entanglement across rostrum and on peduncle and fluke insertion. Resights indicate healing.
No gear present but new, extensive entanglement injuries to peduncle and leading edge of fluke. Resights show injuries healing and reproductively successful.

Free-swimming with bullet buoy lodged in right mouthline, far forward. Line seen exiting left gape. No trailing gear visible. Full configuration unknown, but in poor condition—emaciated with heavy cyamid load. No resights.
At least 25 new, minor, healing lacerations along right flank. Resights indicate healing.
Free-swimming with 2 polyballs trailing 30 ft. aft of flukes. Attachment point(s) unknown. No photos. No resights.
New, healing abrasion to right blowhole with new, minor, healed lacerations running aft along back. Resights indicate healing.
No gear present. New, raw, and healing injuries along leading edges of both flukes and on peduncle consistent with entanglement. Resights indicate healing. Event added in 2018-2022 report.

Dependent calf with two separate vessel strike injuries—a deep laceration to head with evidence of healing indicating it a separate event from the deep, fatal laceration to peduncle. (See 24-Jun-2020 event in Appendix 2 for details.) Event added in 2017-2021 report.
No gear present. Dependent calf with new, healing injuries consistent with constricting body wrap. Resights indicate healing.
No gear present. New, extensive injuries to peduncle and leading edges of flukes consistent with entanglement. Resights indicate healing until subsequent entanglement (see 13-Jul-2021 event).

column6: 2.7 nm E of Sea Bright, NJ
column11: Free-swimming with 2 lines embedded in rostrum, remaining configuration unknown. Extremely poor condition—emaciated, gray skin, large open lesion on left side of head. No resights.

column6: 9.1 nm E of Fernandina Beach, FL
column11: Free-swimming with constricting wraps at peduncle and fluke insertion and around left fluke blade. No resights post 12-Jan-2021.

column6: 27 nm E of Cumberland Island, GA
column11: Fifty-four ft. vessel traveling at 28 kn. self-reported strike. Lactating female with lacerations of unknown depth resighted on 16-Feb-2021. Dependent calf died from injuries received (see 12-Feb-2021 event in Appendix 2). No additional resights.
Two minor propeller lacerations to right fluke tip. Subsequent sightings show eventual loss of tip, but evidence of healing. Event added in 2018-2022 report.

Free-swimming with constricting rostrum wrap and trailing gear 300 ft. Partial disentanglements on 3 separate occasions removed sections of trailing gear. Successful calving event in December 2021. Stable health until 23-Jul-2022 when appeared thinner, increased lesions and cyamids, and discolored rostrum. Sighted 22-Sep-2022 in significant health decline and carrying new entanglement. No additional resights. Dependent calf (see 02-Dec-2021 event) last sighted on 26-Apr-2022. Originally reported as Unknown, First seen USA and LOF Yes. Updated in 2018-2022 report.

column6: 11.3 nm N of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: No gear present but new, extensive, raw and healing injuries along peduncle and at fluke insertion. Resights indicate healing.

column6: 32.6 nm NE of Sainte Marie - Saint Raphael, QC
column11: Recent (within 5 hours) entanglement. Line through mouth and over rostrum leading down towards right flipper and back towards flukes. Constricting line over peduncle and down to weighted gear. Resighted on 14-Jul-2021 with rostrum wrap leading down to weighted gear, no gear on fluke or peduncle area. No additional resights.
Dependent calf of entangled lactating female (see 10-Mar-2021 event). No resights post 26-Apr-2022. Originally reported as Unknown, First seen USA and LOF Yes. Updated in 2018-2022 report.
No gear present. New, healing rostrum injury consistent with constricting gear. Resights indicate healing.

Free-swimming with line through mouth, possibly forming a bridle under the chin, and trailing aft. 1 portion trails hundreds of feet, terminating at a bullet buoy and polyball, the other portion descends to depth. Full configuration unknown. No resights.
At least 8 healing and healed lacerations of unknown depth across back. Resights indicate injuries healing.
Free-swimming with tight body wrap. Attachment point(s) unknown. No additional resights post-07-Jul-2022. Previously reported as 30-Jun-2022.
No gear present, but raw and healing injuries on head, lips, peduncle, and flukes consistent with constricting entanglement. Resights indicate healing.
Cryptic entanglement, line shifting during observation period. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear free and healing. Previously reported as Unknown; First seen Canada.

Free-swimming with line through mouth and trailing to weighted gear. Possible flipper involvement. Heavy cyamid load on mandibles, lips, and head. Still carrying constricting rostrum wrap from previous entanglement (see 10-Mar-2021 event). No resights.

column6: 1 nm E of Ponte Vedra Beach, FL
column11: No gear present. New, raw & healing entanglement injuries to peduncle and leading edges of flukes. Resights indicate healing.
Free-swimming with at least one constricting rostrum wrap. Line exits both sides of mouth, entending back to constricting peduncle wraps and trailing aft of flukes. No resights.

column6: 19.0 nm SE of St. Simons Island, GA
column11: Free-swimming with open bridle—line exiting both sides of mouth and trailing aft of pectoral along the right side and 300+ ft. along left side. Partial disentanglement—trailing line shortened. Resights confirm shed remaining gear and healing.

column6: 7.8 nm SE of Surf City, NC
column11: Free-swimming with embedded peduncle wraps. Disentangled, but remains SI due to health decline—swimming with flippers only and heavy cyamid load. No resights.

Free-swimming. Line exiting right mouth and trailing eventually became three embedded body wraps and contricting wraps on left pectoral. Partial disentanglement—200 ft. of line removed, body wraps shifted aft, constricting pectoral wraps remained. Significant health decline—heavy cyamid load and compromised flipper. Seen with subsequent new entanglement (see 30-Jul-2023 event).

column6: 3.7 nm S of Tybee Island, GA
column11: Two new, superficial, healing lacerations at forward left part of peduncle. Resights indicate healing.
No gear present. New rostrum, lip, and peduncle injuries consistent with constricting entanglement. Resights indicate healing.
No gear present. New wrapping rostrum injury consistent with constricting entanglement. Resights indicate healing.
No gear present. New, extensive injuries across rostrum, inside mouth, on peduncle, and leading edges of flukes consistent with constricting gear. Resights indicate healing.
No gear present. New body, chin, pectoral, peduncle, and fluke injuries consistent with constricting entanglement. Resights indicate healing.

No gear visible. Evidence of new, constricting rostrum wraps and extensive injuries to peduncle and flukes. Right pectoral pale and compromised indicating constricting gear. Extreme health decline—emaciated, large lesions on head, and moderate cyamid load. No resights post-13-Jun-2023.

column6: 25.1 nm SE of Miscou Island, NB
column11: Free-swimming with line exiting left mouth line, passing over left flipper, then under body and trailing 3-4 body lengths beyond right fluke blade. No wraps. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.
No gear present. New, wrapping rostrum injury consistent with constricting entanglement. Resights indicate healing.
No gear present. New extensive entanglement injuries along peduncle and fluke insertion. Resights indicate healing.

No gear present. New entanglement injuries on peduncle and fluke, including sheared leading edge of right fluke blade with distal tip partly amputated. Resights show tip fully amputated and wounds healing.

column6: 22.3 nm SE of Miscou Island, NB
column11: Free-swimming with at least three constricting head wraps trailing to sinking line. Already carrying gear from previous entanglement (see 09-Feb-2023 event). Continued health decline. No resights post-24-Aug-2023.
No gear present. New, wrapping rostrum and fluke insertion injuries consistent with constricting entanglement. Resights indicate healing.

column4: Gulf of Maine humpback whale (Megaptera novaeangliae)
column11: # events/sums:

column6: 0.5 nm S of Cape May, NJ
column11: Free-swimming with constricting line across back, remaining configuration and attachment point(s) unknown. No resights post 01-Jun-2019.

column6: Avalon Pier, Kill Devil Hills, NC
column11: Free-swimming with a large, deep wound on back. Propeller lacerations along edge of wound. Injury healing, and animal appears in good condition. No resights.

column6: 0.1 nm W of Ocean City, MD
column11: Partially disentangled from anchoring gear. Left free-swimming with gear through mouth at minimum. Final configuration unknown. No resights.

15 fresh, superficial lacerations along right side and across peduncle. Resights indicate healing. Subsequently sighted with new fluke injuries (see 22-Aug-2019 event). Event added in 2018-2022 report.

column6: 11.5 nm N of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Free-swimming with thin line over right fluke. Attachment point(s) unknown. Shed gear during observation. Resights indicate healing.
Free-swimming with constricting line across back. Partial disentanglement, but final configuration unknown. Evidence of significant health decline—thin with moderate cyamid load. No resights.
Struck by 48 ft. vessel traveling at 20 kn. Blood seen in water and rudder damaged. No resights.
Caught outside of a set net with netting and floats over the back and dorsal fin, full configuration unknown. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.

column6: 12 mi NE of Shinnecock Inlet, Long Island, NY
column11: Anchored but broke out of gear. All gear recovered. No resights.
Free-swimming with line and buoy trailing past flukes. Partial disentanglement by public. Final configuration unknown. No resights.
Dependent calf. Free-swimming with monofilament on right flipper. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.

column6: 4.5 nm E of Sea Isle City, NJ
column11: Appeared anchored with line wrapped around body and buoy near flipper. Remaining configuration unknown. Confirmed to have shed gear. No additional resights.
Free-swimming with hook attached to leading edge of left fluke blade and monofilament trailing. No wraps. Resights confirm shed gear and healing.
Free-swimming with fishing lure lodged just below the right side of its dorsal fin. No line present. Resights confirm gear-free and injury healing.

column6: 7.4 nm NW of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Free-swimming with monofilament trailing off flukes. Attachment point(s) forward on the body, full configuration unknown. Resights confirm gear-free and reproductively successful.
No gear visible, but evidence of extensive, constricting entanglement with subsequent health decline. No resights post-10-Aug-2019.

Free-swimming with line through the mouth—line exiting right mouthline crossed over the head just aft of blowholes and twisting together with line exiting left mouthline. Unclear if constricting but likely to become so. Left line ended at buoy, right line ended at gangion. Disentangled. Injuries healing until subsequent entanglement (see 03-Jun-2020 event).

Large, raw, injuries bisecting both fluke blades. Human-caused, but no expert consensus on source. Limited resights show increased cyamid load. Carcass documented on 09-Oct-2019, but no necropsy conducted to determine CODd. Event added in 2018-2022 report. Value against PBR in Table 5 apportioned as 0.5 entanglement and 0.5 vessel strike.
Free-swimming with buoy near left flipper. Attachment point(s) unknown. No resights post-19-Oct-2019.

column6: 1.4 nm W of North Truro, MA
column11: Free-swimming with line over back and buoy close to body. Attachment point(s) unknown. Confirmed shed gear. New vessel strike injury also seen (see 03-Sep-2019 event below). Carcass found 14-Nov-2019, no COD determined.

column6: 1.4 nm W of North Truro, MA
column11: New, minor, healing propeller lacerations. Also carrying gear (see 03-Sep-2019 event above). Carcass found 14-Nov-2019, no COD determined.
No gear present, but evidence of extensive, recent, constricting entanglement. Healing injuries on head, body, peduncle, and fluke insertion wraps. No resights.

Free-swimming with anchoring system through the mouth—buoy line exiting the right mouthline and anchor line out the left. Full configuration unclear. Partial disentanglement—left with clean length of line through mouth or line with anchor portion, either of which likely to shed. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.

column6: 8.3 nm NW of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Free-swimming with monofilament trailing from right fluke blade. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear-free and reproductively successful.
Free-swimming in weir without netting. Released. No resights.

column6: 2.2 nm W of North Truro, MA
column11: Free-swimming but dragging gear with a constricting wrap around head/body. Partial disentanglement—left with loose configuration that appears to have shed, but unable to confirm. No resights.
Free-swimming with short length of loose monofilament on right fluke tip. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.

column6: 14.4 nm SE of Ocean City, MD
column11: Free-swimming with 2 buoys near flukes and remaining configuration unclear. Partial disentanglement. Final configuration unknown. No resights.

column6: 6.2 nm N of Brier Island, NS
column11: Anchored by tail. Partial disentanglement—free-swimming with balloon at left flipper and buoy along right side. Remaining gear later found adrift. Resights indicate healing.
Vessel <65 ft. and traveling <10 kn. witnessed striking animal along left flank. No injuries observed. Animal also carrying gear (see 29-May-2020 event below). No resights.
Free-swimming with heavy red netting in mouth. Full configuration unknown. Also struck by a vessel earlier in the day (see 29-May-2020 event above). No resights.
Free-swimming with high flyer on rostrum, line through mouth, and over left flipper. Unable to determine full configuration. Partially disentangled. Final configuration unknown. No resights.
Whale breached and landed on 22 ft. stationary vessel. Significant boat damage. No resights.
Free-swimming with line and buoy trailing 75 ft. aft of flukes. Attachment point(s) unknown. No photos. No resights.

column6: 0.5 nm E of North Truro, MA
column11: Free-swimming with bullet buoy trailing. Attachment point(s) unknown. No photos. No resights.

column6: 6.6 nm NW of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Event witnessed. Lactating female struck monofilament rod and reel. Fisher cut line. Line trailing over right fluke but attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.

Free-swimming with constricting wrap on right fluke blade. No forward attachment points. One short trailing line and another descending to depth. Partially disentangled—left with non-constricting loop. Resights confirm shed gear and healing.

column6: 5.8 nm SW of East Atlantic Beach, NY
column11: Anchored by flukes in extremely heavy gear including trawl net and braided 1/2 in. steel cable. Gear wrapped both fluke blades and embedded into leading edge. Disentangled. Resights indicate healing.
Free-swimming with hook on leading edge of left fluke blade and monofilament trailing. No wraps. Resights confirm shed gear and healing.

column6: N of Hospital Island, St. Andrews, NB
column11: Entrapped in weir for at least 2 days. Released with no apparent injuries. No resights.

Free-swimming with line exiting both sides of mouth, 1 loose loop aft of blowholes and evidence of line embedded in blowholes. 2 lines trail along right flank ending in a jumble 15 ft. aft of flukes. Poor condition—emaciated with gray skin. No resights.

column6: 5.4 nm E of Plum Island, NJ
column11: Free-swimming with constricting wraps at fluke insertion and trailing gear. Full configuration unknown. No resights.

column6: 1 nm N of Montauk Inlet, East Hampton, NY
column11: Free-swimming with line over back and buoy along left flank. Attachment point(s) unknown. Animal dove and buoy with 70 ft. of line came to surface. Brief sightings showed no gear trailing from flukes. Likely shed gear but unable to confirm. No resights.

column6: 12.7 nm ESE of Cape May, NJ
column11: Free-swimming trailing buoy aft of flukes. Attachment point(s) unknown. Emaciated with cyamids. No resights.

column6: 15.3 nm S of Grand Manan, NB
column11: Anchored by flukes with multiple constricting body wraps. Partial disentanglement—all body wraps cut and anchoring lines cut, but unable to confirm gear-free. No resights.

column6: 2 nm E of Virginia Beach, VA
column11: Free-swimming trailing bullet buoy one body length aft of flukes. Attachment point(s) forward on body but full configuration unknown. Confirmed gear-free. No resights post 11-Jan-2021

column6: 10.0 nm SE of Bald Head Island, SC
column11: Evidence of 2 sets of propeller lacerations on flukes: healed lacerations on left distal end and lacerations likely affecting major artery on right fluke.  In extremely poor condition—emaciated, gray skin, heavy cyamid load. No resights.
Free-swimming with tight body wrap of heavy line just forward of flippers. No resights.

Dependent calf. Free-swimming with embedded body wrap. Only visible gear were short bitter ends emerging from chest. Attempted disentanglement—unsure if cutting arrows made contact with line. Resights confirm shed gear and healing until subsequent entanglement (see 08-Aug-2021 event).

column6: 7.6 nm NE of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Free-swimming in thin condition with buoy trailing 100 ft. aft of flukes. Attachment point(s) unknown. Relocated 09-Jun-2021 anchored with line exiting left gape and descending, the other end exiting right mouth. Disentangled. Resights indicate healing.
Free-swimming with small piece of monofilament netting caught on tip of right flipper. No wraps. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.
Free-swimming with monofilament over back. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights indicate gear-free and healing until subsequent entanglement (see 04-Sep-2022 event).
Dependent calf with 4 new, superficial lacerations along right flank. Involved in subsequent vessel strike 2 days later (see 30-Jun-2021 event).

column6: 14.2 nm SE of Block Island, RI
column11: Free-swimming wrapped in netting but no photos or description to determine extent or configuration of entanglement. No resights.

Dependent calf surfaced under stern of 89.5 ft. vessel at idle speed. Blood observed coming from flukes. Documented on 01-Jul-2021 with 4-5 fresh lacerations along right trailing edge of fluke, to the right of the fluke notch. No resights post 20-Oct-2021.

column6: 12.9 nm NE of Race Point, Provincetown, RI
column11: Dependent calf. Free-swimming with closed bridle of monofilament through mouth with line along right side, trailing aft of fluke at depth and along left flank, and crossing over peduncle. Partial disentanglement—shortened line on both sides leaving calf with open bridle. Resights confirm gear-free and healing until subsequent entanglement (see 08-Aug-2021 event).

column6: 7.3 nm NW of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Free-swimming with monofilament line trailing from right fluke tip. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.

column6: 6.5 nm NW of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Witnessed animal strike gear from rod and reel fisher. No photos or description of attachment point(s) or configuration. No resights.
Free-swimming with monofilament trailing from left fluke. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.

Free-swimming, emaciated, with heavy cyamid load all over back. Evidence of multiple constricting body wraps at mid-body. Unable to confirm if gear is embedded. Deep laceration at right pectoral insertion consistent with constricting gear. No resights.

column6: 3.0 nm NW of Brier Island, NS
column11: Free-swimming with monofilament trailing over right fluke blade. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.
Free-swimming with monofilament line trailing over left fluke blade. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.
Free-swimming with constricting wraps at fluke insertion. No resights.

column6: 14.1 nm N of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Lactating female. Free-swimming with monofilament trailing from barnacle on left tip. No wraps. Resights confirm gear-free and reproductively successful.
Dependent calf. Free-swimming with line looped over leading edge of left pectoral and trailing 2 buoys aft of fluke. Witnessed shed gear. Resights indicate injuries healing.
Dependent calf. Free-swimming with monofilament trailing from flukes—line passing over left fluke blade, under peduncle, and then over right fluke blade. Shed gear. No resights post 09-Aug-2021.
Free-swimming with line crossing over back, just forward of flippers and trailing aft of flukes. Full configuration unknown. No resights.

column6: 35.9 nm E of Ashbury Park, NJ
column11: Free-swimming with netting wrapping body, 2 lines embedded forward of dorsal. Extremely poor condition—heavy cyamid load. No resights.
New, superficial lacerations below left dorsal fin. No resights.
Free-swimming in extremely poor condition: flukes missing, vertebrae exposed, emaciated, heavy cyamid load. No gear present, but injury consistent with constricting entanglement. No resights.

column6: 14.1 nm E of Hampton Beach, NH
column11: Free-swimming with hook in trailing edge of dorsal fin and trailing monofilament aft of flukes. No wraps. Resights confirm gear-free and healing until subsequent entanglement (see 04-Jul-2022 event).

column6: 2.0 nm E of Little Island, VA
column11: Free-swimming with line embedded forward of dorsal. Line extending forward on both sides, indicating mouth and/or flipper involvement, but full configuration unknown. No resights.

Free-swimming lactating female sighted on calving grounds with jumble of line across back and remaining configuration unknown. Injuries indicate constricting body wrap at earlier point. Resighted in May on feeding grounds. No gear visible, but unable to confirm gear free. Both female and dependent calf (see 12-Mar-2022 event below) in poor health - emaciated with scattered cyamids. No resights post 06-May-2022
Dependent calf of seriously injured entangled lactating female (see 12-Mar-2022 event above). Both animals show evidence of health decline. No resights post 06-May-2022.

Free-swimming lactating female with 5 body wraps around body, forward of dorsal fin with a mass of line along left flank and no trailing gear. In poor condition - thin with moderate cyamids. Disentangled, short section of gear recovered. Resighted in Sep-2022, calf not present. Additional sightings indicate healing.

Dependent calf of female entangled in multiple body wraps and in poor condition. Adult disentangled and seen later in improving condition but no resights of the calf.  (See 30-May-2022 event above.) Suspect failure to thrive due to poor condition of adult during key lactation months.

Free-swimming dependent calf with line through mouth leading to a jumble of line at the dorsal peduncle with 2 lines trailing a few feet aft of flukes. Evidence that entanglement involved extensive constricting wraps from head to fluke. Disentangled, but SI warranted due to extensive injuries. No resights.

No gear present. Extensive raw and healing injuries to body, peduncle, and fluke consistent with constricting entanglement. Resights indicate injuries healing. Carcass recovered in 2023, COD unrelated to this event.

column6: 0.7 nm W of Coney Island, NY
column11: Free-swimming with constricting line at fluke insertion. Tight peduncle wrap(s) and line cutting into leading edges of flukes. Little to no trailing gear. No resights

Free-swimming with line trailing along right flank. Indication of constricting wrap aft of blowholes, but no gear visible. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights indicate no evidence of health decline. Likely gear-free but unable to confirm.
Lunge feeding whale came in contact with starboard bow of stationary recreational fishing vessel. No human injuries. No resights.

column6: 2.5 nm N of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Free-swimming with monofilament crossing over back and trailing along right flank. Some hooks in right body. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear free and healing.
Lunge feeding whale landed on bow of stationary 19 ft recreational vessel, damaging railings and hatch cover. No human injuries. Resights indicate healing.
Free-swimming with monofilament trailing from head. Attachment point(s) unknown. No resights.

column6: 0.2 nm N of Long Island, MA
column11: Free-swimming with monofilament trailing from left fluke. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear free.
Free-swimming with hook in trailing edge of right fluke blade. Monofilament trailing. No wraps. No resights.
Free-swimming with hook in right fluke blade and trailing monofilament line. No wraps. Resights indicate hook gone and injuries healing.

column6: 0.8 nm E of North Truro, MA
column11: Free-swimming with 2 buoys trailing 60 ft. aft of flukes. Attachment point(s) unknown. No resights.
Free-swimming with line originating from forward on the left side, crossing over back at dorsal fin, and trailing aft. Attachment point(s) unknown. No resights.

Free-swimming with line through mouth leading to a loose peduncle wrap with 200 ft of trailing line. Orientation - head up and tail down - indicates presence of weighted gear. Disentangled. Resights indicate healing.

column6: 1.3 nm N of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Free-swimming with line through the mouth trailing to dorsal fin on left side and along right side ending at a buoy at the flukes. Evidence that entanglement previously involved a constricting body wrap. Disentanglement attempt unsuccessful. Expect to shed gear. No resights.

column6: 9.3 nm N of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Free-swimming with monofilament trailing from right fluke. Attachment point(s) unknown. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.

column6: 1 nm E of Seaside Heights, NJ
column11: Fresh laceration to fluke blade, just left of midline. Injury actively bleeding. Resights show left fluke lobe missing, but injury healing and animal in good condition.
Lunge feeding whale came in contact with port bow of stationary recreational fishing vessel. No human injuries. No resights.

column6: 0.5 nm S of Jones Beach, NY
column11: Feeding whale surfaced under stationary recreational vessel, dislodging propeller. Animal documented with 2 bleeding, superficial lacerations along left flank, below the dorsal. No human injuries. No resights.

column6: 7.5 nm E of Appledore Island, ME
column11: Anchored with tight body and peduncle wrap with buoy alongside body. Partially disentangled. Final configuration unclear but at minimum free-swimming with tight body wrap and buoy alongside. No resights.

column6: 3.8 nm E of St. Catherine's Island, GA
column11: Free-swimming with constricting wraps at fluke insertion leading to fluke deformation. No resights.
Anchored in extensive wraps of gear from head to tail. Partially disentangled. Remaining configuration unknown. No resights.
New, healing lacerations across back. Do not appear to penetrate blubber layer. No resights.

column6: 38.2 nm E of Atlantic City, NJ
column11: Free-swimming with a minimum of 2 constricting and embedded body wraps. No resights.
Stern of stationary recreational vessel (<45 ft.) struck by lunge feeding whale. No human injuries. No resights.

column6: 19.8 nm SW of Grand Manan, NB
column11: Anchored by line through mouth. Disentangled, resights indicate healing.

column6: 6.7 nm NW of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Dependent calf. Free-swimming with constricting wrap cutting deeply into leading and trailing edges of left pectoral. Resights indicate line cutting 3/4 of the way through the flipper and declining health. Seen with a new entanglement (see 20-Sep-2023 event).

column6: 2.1 nm E of Sandy Hook, NJ
column11: Free-swimming with line over back and trailing with two buoys. Unable to confirm full configuration. Resights confirm gear-free and healing.

column6: 1.2 nm S of Cape May, NJ
column11: Free-swimming, emaciated with webbing on head. Full configuration unknown. SI warranted because of evidence of health decline. No resights post-19-Jul-2023

column6: 8.7 nm S of Boothbay Harbor, ME
column11: Hogtied in gear with line through mouth extending back to multiple constricting peduncle wraps. Partially disentangled. Left with open bridle through mouth that expect to shed. No resights.

column6: 6.6 nm NW of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Dependent calf. Free-swimming with line through the mouth and over head with unknown attachment point(s). Partially disentangled—shortened trailing lines leaving an open bridle. Resights confirm shed gear and healing.

column6: 6.1 nm NW of Race Point, Provincetown, MA
column11: Free-swimming, pregnant female with monofilament trailing from right fluke blade with unknown attachment point(s). Resights confirm shed gear and reproductively successful.

Free-swimming with line exiting left mouthline, crossing over the head, and descending to mouth or right flipper. One bitter end visible, the other sinking. Full configuration unknown.
